mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns within one week as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 20-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST grow concurrently over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery phase indicate capacity utilization improvements, generating positive quarterly equity returns as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are flat or negative during recovery phases when INDPRO rises, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on corporate leverage, causing immediate negative equity repricing within one week as liquidity premiums widen.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns through wage-driven consumption growth.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows zero marginal predictive power relative to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline of 100 during a late-cycle expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal fails to distinguish late-cycle supply constraints from other regimes.
